LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE OCT-16-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO THE SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGED AGAINST IT IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF BRADFORD SOLINSKY IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE, IF SOLINKSY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THAT THE CITY IS EQUITABLY ESTOPPED FROM ASSERTING THE STATUTE REQUIRING THAT SOLINSKY FILE THIS ACTION AGAINST THE CITY WITHIN SIX MONTHS OF THE CITY'S DENIAL OF HIS GOVERNMENT CODE CLAIM. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/HEK)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-29-2026 Set For Trial On Oct-26-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Bradford Solinsky is overruled. Solinsky has adequately alleged that the City is equitably estopped from asserting that Solinsky's claim is untimely filed. The fact that the City informed Solinsky of the applicable time requirements does not preclude the application of equitable estoppel. Order signed. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-19-2025 Continued To Feb-11-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-02-2025 Continued To Dec-10-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-16-2025 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the sole cause of action alleged against it in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Bradford Solinsky is sustained with 20 days leave to amend to allege, if Solinksy can do so in good faith, that the City is equitably estopped from asserting the statute requiring that Solinsky file this action against the City within six months of the City's denial of his Government Code claim. Relying on facts not alleged in his first amended complaint, Solinsky argues that the statute of limitations as to the City is equitably tolled. It appears, however, that Solinsky seeks to invoke the doctrine of equitable estoppel (CACI 456; see generally J.P. v. Carlsbad Unified School District (2014) 232 Cal. App. 4th 323, 333 (in affirming a tort claim verdict against a government entity, the court, quoting a 1989 California Supreme Court case, stated "It is well settled that a public entity may be estopped from asserting the limitations of the claims statute where its agents or employees have prevented or deterred the filing of a timely claim by some affirmative act")) rather than the related but distinct doctrine of equitable tolling. Based on the statements in his opposition memorandum, while this court does not determine whether Solinsky is able to adequately allege equitable estoppel, he should be given the opportunity to do so.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/HEK)
